| | Dear Fair Fight Family, Georgia is full of warriors who can, together, overcome the toughest obstacles. We know that you are one of those warriors, and, even amid a global pandemic, you will fight with us to make sure every voice is heard. Today, Georgia's Secretary of State announced the state will move the presidential primary to May 19 -- the same date as the non-presidential primaries. There are a lot of questions about this change, but please know that no matter what, Fair Fight will work hard so that democracy is protected. The Secretary of State's office has committed that Georgians who have already cast their vote for the March 24 primary will have their votes counted; however, if you voted already, you can vote again in the May 19 primary to participate in down-ballot races. Fair Fight will be with you every step of the way to make sure your vote counts. Stay tuned on election updates by following us on [Twitter](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42b/34a064cc/1b5ce57f/702b9c9/3726113371/VEsE/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://twitter.com/fairfightaction"), [Instagram](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42b/34a064cc/1b5ce57f/702b9d6/3726113371/VEsF/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://www.instagram.com/fairfightaction/?hl=en"), and [Facebook](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42b/34a064cc/1b5ce57f/702b9d7/3726113371/VEsC/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://www.facebook.com/FairFightAction/"). Below is the statement from Fair Fight's Senior Advisor Lauren Groh-Wargo: "Fair Fight is proud of the record number of Georgia Democrats and record number of Georgians of color who participated in early voting for this primary, demonstrating once again that Georgia will be a pivotal battleground state in November. Moving the presidential primary to May 19 will allow even more Georgians to participate, and Fair Fight will be watching to ensure every vote is properly counted." Since the founding of Fair Fight, we have known (and shared with the world!) that Georgia is a pivotal battleground state. Your vote is incredibly important, and we are committed to making sure your voice is heard. Together, we will make history in 2020 and ensure every Georgian can have the freedom and opportunity to thrive. Please stay tuned with us as we continue to fight for your right to vote. With gratitude, #TeamFairFight |

None of this is easy. This is a moment that will define us - as individuals, as leaders, and as a country. It will test all of us, and the choices we make and how we show up, really matters. I know you are pushing through and doing your best. Demonstrati | | [ ](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/-QA/ni0YAA/t.303/C5WDbXB6SQCmIt5QoKhFJg/h0/EITbMdFK8sEmH288ys-2B-2Fh8ph-2FrB9A6-2BM0i5qCb2xsCyiK7xXkJftbeIVM7HbMWah-2F-2FTUy7T-2FpE-2Ban7TzilxtVHMJk6Ko3XMudiK22ctjhsmRptTnUeLyseIwv7dBH718Y4MbjYu-2FB2IfsFMX3fd2Wc3GM7Ai-2BUP120SuP-2BsGpEOJ6eh9NA9-2FiBlTBwGFOpB-2FuntDb-2BrZixohux1nYVtppg9ikH4r8mAyr7rtmw0s-2FtaNRSR3iwQ06IjZz0a2WfF5fEkg8dJMxkQaJIn0YYtMN4qTAiFH6g2KsXXZjLPJHt0-3D/jj4p)

Dear Friend, I want to take a few minutes to talk with you about the coronavirus and public health emergency that Oregon and much of the country are now experiencing. This is a very serious situation and I want to share my perspective, my plans, and how we can stick together in this challenging time. First, I have heard your concerns. I hear you are troubleshooting childcare and wondering where children who rely on school for meals will eat. I hear that you are concerned about accessing healthcare. I hear the reports of anti-asian racism on campus and in our community. Top of mind for me is our unhoused community, and our elders and those with underlying conditions who are most at risk. And all of us, whoever we are, are feeling the stress of uncertainty. None of this is easy. This is a moment that will define us - as individuals, as leaders, and as a country. It will test all of us, and the choices we make and how we show up, really matters. I know you are pushing through and doing your best. Demonstrating strength, flexibility, and resilience to care for yourself, your family, and our community. My highest priority is the safety and wellbeing of our community, and I remain as committed as ever to my vision, our vision, of healthcare for all. We are flexible, resourceful, and adaptable - we are pivoting into this moment, and we will continue to organize and lead. Now is the time for us to unite across our differences and make policy choices that help every-day people -- not the richest 1% and a handful of corporations. As individuals, we can wash our hands, avoid unnecessary travel and check in on our friends and neighbors. As a united force, **we can demand our local, state and national governments do everything possible to protect everyone 's health, no exceptions.**
---

**Here are the steps the Canning for Congress campaign is taking: **
* We are in touch with our supporters, many of you are community leaders in your areas, about your concerns and about solutions and resources. We will do all we can to use our platforms to amplify those and keep our coalition connected and strong.
* To reach voters, we are expanding our phone banking program and peer to peer texting. While voters are at home doing social distancing, volunteers like you can be calling them to chat, keeping people company and talking about the importance of this upcoming election for policies like Medicare for All.
* We will also be hosting virtual events - the first of which is our **[Democratic debate watch party Sunday evening at 5 PM on Facebook.](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/-QA/ni0YAA/t.303/C5WDbXB6SQCmIt5QoKhFJg/h6/Nejq3qQQ6Jn1R8HM5bnp8LuTy-2Bwqi23tDCLge9VhpImWHicgyonUVCWHOtuQFzQ3LCt3dEO37zG93LnnIp3y3kSuHcFzM-2FzKq-2B77htwpQNDpMvK4aap8unATS2sge7QvW1tPNU6qHjJuG5yfRup0lUiEapJGe9qyoGYEu2YK2tZ8OglKSmD3dmPZsWX5Qrcei42z7U-2FIMXW7pPv520tU1hF0hzxIt0AW5TSZ1CcMriLpvsYCmE9XNa6mJXuzcLdaeXwPkdj51bvqduSG-2FoXv1gKrMGXqCVcjmyzCDNGEckIXYTQ1TIatXvRoliovP-2BFS/Gepe)** So tune in, invite your friends, and spread the word!

name: Jason Yates
subject: It’s happening this morning
body:
| | Good morning Peter, Did you see my email yesterday? Our President has declared TODAY as a National Day of Prayer. In doing so, he reminded the entire country that throughout its history, the United States has looked to God for strength and protection in times like these. Let’s remember that today. It’s time to pray and replace fear with faith. In light of the many concerns about the Coronavirus, many churches have canceled their services today. If that’s true for your church, please consider joining us LIVE at **10:30 am CT today** for an online service we are calling “America, It’s Time to Pray.” This online service will be a meaningful time of worship, teaching, and prayer. The event is starting soon. I hope you will join us. name: Rita Hart for Congress
subject: Campaign update regarding COVID-19
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa
body:
| | | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/FEqQxyQgQDvyuPICFWQGspAthyfMXpZbLKC-z7TY0mY=/83pCYf4iZLkPHivff2p4KZ2OXZW5FGg2z3mzkXL7Wd8=) The safety and health of Iowans is our first priority so, during this uncertain time, we want to let you know that we are postponing our district travel and will be moving our campaign events to virtual options for the time being. Per the recommendations of the CDC, our team will be teleworking while Paul and Rita work from the farm. Iowa has confirmed cases of COVID-19, or the coronavirus, in multiple counties and confirmed community spread. We want to make sure you're all staying safe. Please follow Iowa's resources for dealing with the coronavirus here: [https://idph.iowa.gov/emerging-health-issues/novel-coronavirus](https://act.myngp.com/el/FEqQxyQgQDvyuPICFWQGspAthyfMXpZbLKC-z7TY0mY=/83pCYf4iZLkPHivff2p4KZ2OXZW5FGg2z3mzkXL7Wd8=) Here are a few recommendations from the CDC as we work to keep our at-risk communities safe:
* Wash your hands for at least 20 seconds with antibacterial soap.
* Avoid touching your face.
* Use hand sanitizer regularly after interacting with public spaces.
* Avoid large crowds.
* Practice social distancing, if able. This means making a conscious effort to reduce close contact between people, so try to work remotely if you can, avoid crowds, and avoid close quarters with others.
* If you're in an at-risk community -- those with heart disease, lung disease, immunodeficiency or the elderly -- avoid travel and social interaction when possible.
* If you begin to experience symptoms, please limit contact with others and contact your doctor. Most people will experience coronavirus with minor symptoms, so if you feel ill, please stay at home until you feel well. This will help protect our at-risk communities by letting our health care providers focus on those who need it most.

Thank you for helping us keep our Iowa communities safe. \-- Rita Hart for Congress | Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa

body:
We started this campaign because the change we dream of and talk about will never happen unless we start doing things very differently.
#
I have a big favor to ask you.
[**Will you chip in before our mid-month fundraising deadline?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16126648/170949309/-608415112?refcode=em20200315&amount=18.50&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MzdiOTJmZi1mYzY2LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DExiPQCcd-44plpkgtR-uO3q6mO0XWVNdY2cnI6XOqc=) We need to raise $4,000 by midnight to stay on track and keep our campaign’s programs fully funded for the month.
**[Donate $18.50
_(average donation this week)_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16126649/170949310/-608415112?refcode=em20200315&amount=18.50&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MzdiOTJmZi1mYzY2LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DExiPQCcd-44plpkgtR-uO3q6mO0XWVNdY2cnI6XOqc=)**
We started this campaign because the change we dream of and talk about will never happen unless we start doing things very differently.
If we want to kick Mitch out of the Senate, we won’t win by running to the center and acting like a Republican. We need to embrace our progressive values & offer people a new kind of politics.

body:

[ ]( https://4gzmh.r.ag.d.sendibm3.com/mk/cl/f/1P3_XZQUfpUe-qu5QTbXsVXAkMJ-a2rfvbYW-muVfi5WkcFIpYyHgILGWTYyF2SVatDQRjsHW1hsst8bsUaFzZ3zmx4aeQlMAxXI0mhD4lAFQLbvxtNHsN_CkDHLtV-yMmQdiugEldRO_sfBQ_Cjp-__vfj2_fPWSEjlyBuJXjuEghH8ikd0TIdF )
>From Your Capitol - March 15, 2020

This week was a busy week for the Mississippi legislature. Thursday, March 12 was the deadline for representatives to discuss these general bills. Any bills not discussed in session by this deadline died on the calendar.

On Wednesday evening, the first case of the Corona Virus in Mississippi was reported in Forrest County, and by the end of the week, several more cases had been announced. Speaker of the House Philip Gunn, Speaker Pro Tempore Jason White and Public Health Chairman Sam Mims attended a press conference Thursday morning led by Dr. Thomas Dobbs, the State Health Officer of the Mississippi State Department of Health. At the present time, there are no plans to suspend business in the Legislature. However, all tours at the Capitol have been suspended until further notice.

If you are like me, you are amazed at the panic that is taking place over this virus. Our nation has experienced other illnesses and we have always survived without shutting down our nation. I remember Ebola, Swine Flu, and Bird Flu, all of were concerning and created a degree of caution but none created the panic we see today.

The politics is concerning with the recent panic over the Corona Virus. I caution us all to not give up freedoms in exchange for a false sense of security. Do not for one-second believe that the government can keep you safe or take care of you. We don't need the Governor or the President to issue an order to close schools or businesses. You can keep your kids home from school if you feel it is the safest decision, you can stay home from work or church if you feel it is best. We do not need a politician to make those decisions for us. We must not give up our freedom.

Mississippi House of Representatives Turns Left

The 2020 Mississippi legislative session has seen the Mississippi House of Representatives take a hard turn to the left. We have raised taxes and increase regulation at a rate that should make any democrat proud.

Here are just a few of the worst bills passed so far;

HB265 - raises current salaries for statewide elected officers. There is no shortage of people who seek these jobs so why should the people of Mississippi increase their salary?

H379 – The Facebook Marketplace Tax. This bill authorizes the state to tax, regulate and audit online "marketplace facilitators" (companies that facilitate the online buying and selling of personal property). It also includes a provision that allows this tax to be passed on to those who purchase items through the facilitator. This bill is an attempt by the state government to become involved in the growing online market of personal property.

HB565 - This bill authorizes the issuance of bonds to fund the repair and renovation of the North Oak Regional Medical Center in Senatobia. The state should not borrow money to bailout a private company. This bill was nothing more than a powerful chairman in the Mississippi legislature using his position to bring home money to his district for personal gain. This is dysfunctional government at it's worst.

HB837 – Your Mississippi House of Representative passed this bill that will require the people of Mississippi to pay for same-sex female couples to have children. HB837 mandates that insurance providers in the state cover artificial insemination for couples wanting to have children. This procedure is expensive and will raise the cost of healthcare for everyone. Also, the state cannot discriminate because of sexual orientation which means state employees who are “married” to same sex partners can have the people of Mississippi pay to help them get pregnant.

name: Jason Yates
subject: A National Call to Prayer - Download the Guide
body:
| | Peter, We are grateful for President Trump’s leadership to designate today as a National Day of Prayer due to the Coronavirus pandemic. This is a great reminder for us to seek the Lord together in unity. As people of faith, we can pray about this situation not only today, but as long as our communities and our country remain impacted by the virus. **We’ve created a brief yet powerful prayer guide for us to unite in prayer. The guide contains six specific prayer points along with scripture to pray for this unprecedented time and all of those affected.** Get the free COVID-19 Prayer Guide here: [https://MyFaithVotes.org/covid-19-pandemic-prayer-guide](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ibMzrge1zBfOv81lusjLGlBKTD3CIJFYW7JtL_RvdMIhXPVT_dZRgJSu6jZEK7dQIw&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200315com01_2&n=1) Throughout our history, our nation has turned to God and sought His blessing when we needed it most. This is that time. Let us pray, in the midst of the current turmoil, that we would be a people who seek the Lord, walk with His peace, and love and serve others well. We encourage you to share this prayer guide with your brothers and sisters in Christ. It’s time for our nation to repent, obey, and turn towards God. I want to express my gratitude for you and your faithfulness to remain prayerful during these challenging times. [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ibMzrge1zBfOv81lusjLGlBKTD3CIJFYW7JtL_RvdMIhXPVT_dZRgJSu6jZEK7dQIw&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200315com01_2&n=2) Uniting with you in prayer, Jason Yates

name: Michelle De La Isla
subject: Here's what I have been up to:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MICHELLE FOR KANSAS
body:
I am so grateful to have the opportunity to speak to Kansans face-to-face during my travels across this district.

I am so grateful to have the opportunity to speak to Kansans face-to-face during my travels across this district.

Friends,

One of the most important aspects of this campaign is getting on the road to meet people where they're at, so I can hear firsthand the problems they're facing and make plans for how to address them in Congress. That is why I’m dedicated to visiting all 25 counties in the 2nd District this year -- not only during the campaign -- but later as your new Congresswoman.

In the past few months, I have attended events in Shawnee, Douglas, Jefferson, Nemaha, Atchison, and Franklin Counties. Last weekend, I visited five more counties in southeast Kansas to get a real taste for the issues that are affecting everyday Kansans in some of our rural areas.

The people of southeast Kansas are so wonderful and genuine. They were excited to meet our team, and share their concerns about jobs, healthcare, infrastructure, rural economies, and the needs of their neighbors. Here’s some photos from my trip:
_Michelle talking with residents of Labette and Montgomery Counties at Iron Press_
Last Sunday, I drove to Parsons, Kansas and met with Brian Williams, the CEO of Labette Health to discuss the needs of our rural healthcare networks. After that, we went to the Iron Press for breakfast, and a meet and greet with 40 residents of Labette and Montgomery Counties. After breakfast, I shared my story and answered questions from individuals who have challenges with healthcare, disability rights, and mental health resources. Thank you to Greg Jarrett for arranging our breakfast!
_Michelle speaking at Crawford County Democrats meeting_
Our next stop was Pittsburg, where I started by speaking at the Crawford County Democrats meeting. We had a thoughtful discussion about healthcare and the political landscape of the 2nd District. Crawford County is passionate about finding solutions to our healthcare challenges, and I appreciate the wide variety of opinions that were discussed. I am grateful to Crawford County Chair, Lynn Grant, for allowing me to come and speak to residents.
_Helping stock the shelves for women at TJ Lelands with SEK NOW_
Next, I stopped by to see Deborah McGeorge and SEK NOW's annual fundraising event at TJ Lelands where they were collecting feminine products for local shelters and schools, then I ate an amazing fried chicken dinner with residents from Cherokee County at Chicken Annie’s. Don’t be jealous, Chicken Mary’s - I’ll be stopping by your place the next time I’m in town!

On Monday, I continued my visit to Crawford County by having coffee with Commissioner Jeremy Johnson, Rep. Julie Menghini, and local attorney Fred Spigarelli at Root to discuss issues affecting individuals and families in Crawford County and across the 2nd District.
_Monday morning Coffee at Root with area leadership_

_Meeting with local business owners in downtown Pittsburg_
It was a cold and windy morning, but that didn’t stop Commissioner Johnson and I from enjoying a tour of downtown Pittsburg. I met several local business owners along the way, and learned about the revitalization of downtown Pittsburg over the last few years. Amazing!
_Lunch at Opie’s Pizza in Chanute_
Then I headed north to Neosho County where I stopped at Opie's Pizza in Chanute for lunch with local residents and discussed issues dealing with healthcare, access to rural broadband, education, and gun safety. Thank you for organizing the event, Mark Weaver, the pizza buffet at Opie’s was delicious!
_Coffee break at Around the Corner in Iola_
After lunch, our team got back on the road and traveled north to Iola for coffee with residents at Around the Corner Coffee Shop. We not only enjoyed some really amazing coffee, we also had a great discussion about immigration, healthcare, poverty, sustainable energy, and the natural disasters in Puerto Rico. It meant so much to me that people asked about my family and how they were doing after the fallout of multiple hurricanes and earthquakes on the island. I want to say thank you to Mike Bruner, who helped us organize this event, but could not attend since he was recovering from illness. I’m glad to hear he is feeling better!
_Meeting at the community center in Mound City_
Our last stop of the day was a meet and greet at the Community Center in Mound City. I had a heartfelt discussion with individuals who are concerned about rural farms, healthcare, housing for veterans, gun safety, and many other issues. Thank you, Roger Sims, Councilman Lawrence Forbach, and all who shared their stories with me.

I am so grateful to have the opportunity to speak to so many residents face-to-face, and I’m honored that so many people took time out of their day to stop by one of our events to share their thoughts and concerns. By listening to each other and working together, we can find common sense solutions to the problems Kansans face.

Thanks for being a part of our journey. I am looking forward to our next trip to southeast Kansas, and all across the district, very soon. Stay tuned!

Michelle

clean_body: I am so grateful to have the opportunity to speak to Kansans face-to-face during my travels across this district. I am so grateful to have the opportunity to speak to Kansans face-to-face during my travels across this district. Friends, One of the most important aspects of this campaign is getting on the road to meet people where they're at, so I can hear firsthand the problems they're facing and make plans for how to address them in Congress. That is why I’m dedicated to visiting all 25 counties in the 2nd District this year -- not only during the campaign -- but later as your new Congresswoman. In the past few months, I have attended events in Shawnee, Douglas, Jefferson, Nemaha, Atchison, and Franklin Counties. Last weekend, I visited five more counties in southeast Kansas to get a real taste for the issues that are affecting everyday Kansans in some of our rural areas. The people of southeast Kansas are so wonderful and genuine. They were excited to meet our team, and share their concerns about jobs, healthcare, infrastructure, rural economies, and the needs of their neighbors. Here’s some photos from my trip: Michelle talking with residents of Labette and Montgomery Counties at Iron Press Last Sunday, I drove to Parsons, Kansas and met with Brian Williams, the CEO of Labette Health to discuss the needs of our rural healthcare networks. After that, we went to the Iron Press for breakfast, and a meet and greet with 40 residents of Labette and Montgomery Counties. After breakfast, I shared my story and answered questions from individuals who have challenges with healthcare, disability rights, and mental health resources. Thank you to Greg Jarrett for arranging our breakfast! Michelle speaking at Crawford County Democrats meeting Our next stop was Pittsburg, where I started by speaking at the Crawford County Democrats meeting. We had a thoughtful discussion about healthcare and the political landscape of the 2nd District. Crawford County is passionate about finding solutions to our healthcare challenges, and I appreciate the wide variety of opinions that were discussed. I am grateful to Crawford County Chair, Lynn Grant, for allowing me to come and speak to residents. Helping stock the shelves for women at TJ Lelands with SEK NOW Next, I stopped by to see Deborah McGeorge and SEK NOW's annual fundraising event at TJ Lelands where they were collecting feminine products for local shelters and schools, then I ate an amazing fried chicken dinner with residents from Cherokee County at Chicken Annie’s. Don’t be jealous, Chicken Mary’s - I’ll be stopping by your place the next time I’m in town! On Monday, I continued my visit to Crawford County by having coffee with Commissioner Jeremy Johnson, Rep. Julie Menghini, and local attorney Fred Spigarelli at Root to discuss issues affecting individuals and families in Crawford County and across the 2nd District. Monday morning Coffee at Root with area leadership Meeting with local business owners in downtown Pittsburg It was a cold and windy morning, but that didn’t stop Commissioner Johnson and I from enjoying a tour of downtown Pittsburg. I met several local business owners along the way, and learned about the revitalization of downtown Pittsburg over the last few years. Amazing! Lunch at Opie’s Pizza in Chanute Then I headed north to Neosho County where I stopped at Opie's Pizza in Chanute for lunch with local residents and discussed issues dealing with healthcare, access to rural broadband, education, and gun safety. Thank you for organizing the event, Mark Weaver, the pizza buffet at Opie’s was delicious! Coffee break at Around the Corner in Iola After lunch, our team got back on the road and traveled north to Iola for coffee with residents at Around the Corner Coffee Shop. We not only enjoyed some really amazing coffee, we also had a great discussion about immigration, healthcare, poverty, sustainable energy, and the natural disasters in Puerto Rico. It meant so much to me that people asked about my family and how they were doing after the fallout of multiple hurricanes and earthquakes on the island. I want to say thank you to Mike Bruner, who helped us organize this event, but could not attend since he was recovering from illness. I’m glad to hear he is feeling better! Meeting at the community center in Mound City Our last stop of the day was a meet and greet at the Community Center in Mound City. I had a heartfelt discussion with individuals who are concerned about rural farms, healthcare, housing for veterans, gun safety, and many other issues. Thank you, Roger Sims, Councilman Lawrence Forbach, and all who shared their stories with me. I am so grateful to have the opportunity to speak to so many residents face-to-face, and I’m honored that so many people took time out of their day to stop by one of our events to share their thoughts and concerns. By listening to each other and working together, we can find common sense solutions to the problems Kansans face. Thanks for being a part of our journey. I am looking forward to our next trip to southeast Kansas, and all across the district, very soon. Stay tuned! Michelle **=** PAID FOR BY MICHELLE FOR KANSAS Michelle for Kansas PO Box 67101 Topeka, KS 66667 If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

name: Congressman Al Green
subject: In times of crisis, Americans deserve a real leader
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Keep Al Green in Congress
body:
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/MmYt_p2A-Mj_pLkOoOcmOUbbkZkn4-o7JKv9olgvNrM=/uYzolxa7UYY-qwynYYZfRo_-_q7li5H4vQ1K80LkRcU=)
---
| Folks, With over 1,000 confirmed cases of COVID-19 in the United States and that number rapidly growing, Americans deserve elected leaders who will communicate openly, prepare adequately, and act swiftly to mitigate the effects of this pandemic. But President Trump has proven he is not willing to put the health and safety of millions of at-risk Americans above his ego -- and _**we**_ are the ones who will pay the price. President Trump has:
* Repeatedly downplayed the severity of the crisis, insisting the number of cases in the U.S. is and will stay "close to zero"
* Encouraged those who are sick to continue engaging in risky activities that could fuel the spread of the virus, saying many positive patients "go to work, but they get better"
* Blocked states from using Medicaid funds to support emergency COVID-19 response
* Failed to prepare an adequate number of testing kits and refused to accept any responsibility for problems caused by this failure
* Failed to pass emergency paid leave to slow community spread
* Stood by his proposed 2021 budget, which slashes $1.2 billion from the CDC
* AND, all this after he fired the entire pandemic response team in 2018!

**Folks, it is clear that President Trump is not the leader we need in times of crisis.** In the absence of a real leader, I promise to do everything I can to step up, stand up, and mitigate this public health and safety crisis. I am proud to have supported and voted for an $8.3 billion emergency spending package to address this crisis, which includes funding for state and local agencies, medical equipment, vaccine research, and a clause to waive telehealth restrictions so Medicare beneficiaries can access healthcare from wherever they are. Shortly after midnight on Friday, Congress also passed another relief package which includes paid leave, expanded food assistance, extended unemployment insurance, and free coverage for COVID-19 testing. Right now, my thoughts are with those Americans who still do not have access to quality healthcare, and all those who cannot afford to stay home and give up weeks of pay. My prayers are with all members of vulnerable groups, including the elderly and those with pre-existing respiratory conditions. **I will not stop fighting for you.** Now is the time for clear and honest communication, bold leadership, swift action, and unity. Thanks for standing with me, Congressman Al Green
---
* * *
| | Congressman Al Green has served the people of Texas' 9th district for 15 years. He is a progressive leader in Washington and was the first Member of Congress to introduce articles of impeachment against President Trump on the House floor.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because we want to keep you fully informed about Al's work for justice, accountability, and progress. subject: What this pandemic means for our campaign
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:

| | Peter,

I've dealt with a lot of curveballs in campaigns, but dealing with a pandemic is a new one.

The most important thing to us is keeping our staff and volunteers as safe as possible. To do that, we're canceling lots of in-person events and adjusting our field plan. We're going to have to account for more voters casting their vote by mail rather than going in-person. **And it means the bulk of our fundraising efforts are moving online.**

As Christy told CNN yesterday: "We would like to win, but we don't want to win at the expense of public health."

That means emails like this, asking you for money, are going to become all the more important.

**[Will you pitch in $3 or whatever you can to keep running a strong campaign?](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b817/29776869/af36349/569fa9eb/48194184/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMTVfZGlnaXBpdm90LWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_digipivot?refcode=em20_200315_digipivot-full")** [DONATE](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b817/29776869/af36349/569fa9eb/48194184/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMTVfZGlnaXBpdm90LWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_digipivot?refcode=em20_200315_digipivot-full") Campaigning during this time is not easy -- but during times of crisis, we are reminded of how important reliable leadership is.

We need members of Congress who will constantly demand accountability from the federal government. We need leaders who will fight for affordable health care, easier access to insurance, lower prescription drug prices, and more medical facilities in care deserts.

With how soon our special election is arriving, we can't afford to slow down our efforts -- but we can make adjustments to make everyone safer.

That means cancelling canvassing and acquiring more technology so we can talk to voters on the phones and virtually and spending more money on digital ads.

**[Can you help us out? Your support ensures we can keep running a strong campaign over the next two months, no matter what.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b817/29776869/af36349/569fa9eb/48194184/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMTVfZGlnaXBpdm90LWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_digipivot?refcode=em20_200315_digipivot-full")** [DONATE](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b817/29776869/af36349/569fa9eb/48194184/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMTVfZGlnaXBpdm90LWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_digipivot?refcode=em20_200315_digipivot-full") Thank you for stepping up. Stay safe and healthy.

\-- Brandon

subject: What we need right now...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stand with Sánchez
body:
_****_
**Right now, Congress needs to be doing everything in its power to halt the spread of coronavirus, starting with passing Paid National Sick Leave. **
The House just passed a coronavirus relief bill which includes _some_ paid sick leave for workers. However, depending on how long this global health emergency lasts, it still might not be enough. National Paid Sick Leave would allow the roughly 57 million Americans who do not have paid sick leave to forgo work if sick -- _without_ sacrificing an income.
**Right now, I am co-sponsoring a petition urging Congress to pass National Paid Sick Leave.[Will you help me send my message by signing on?](https://act.myngp.com/el/Z9C2KERFDRlPP4b5HcQ4beANCAyGofkuNz9dCJ5hwgQ=/hs0V0xP7LC5DZdc1ybApsaHB2C25YDXXuUl5hspTBvs=)**
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/Z9C2KERFDRlPP4b5HcQ4beANCAyGofkuNz9dCJ5hwgQ=/_5YbFeJ8H55KQntacHnuRW7vRrP_7hO-7Lx5q48h_XY=)
The United States is the _only_ developed nation without a national policy on paid sick leave. That's because National Paid Sick Leave is proven to keep communities safer and healthier, by allowing those who are sick to stay home.
Even the CDC is recommending for people who feel ill to stay home -- now, it's time we give every working American that same ability.
[**Join me in supporting National Paid Sick Leave | SIGN ON >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/Z9C2KERFDRlPP4b5HcQ4beANCAyGofkuNz9dCJ5hwgQ=/hs0V0xP7LC5DZdc1ybApsaHB2C25YDXXuUl5hspTBvs=)

Thank you for taking action,
\-- _Linda S anchez_

subject: Keeping you informed on the coronavirus
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
As we continue to learn more about the coronavirus (COVID-19), we want to ensure that you have the most recent updates to keep you informed. | | |
---|---|---

Friend,

As Governor Holcomb continues to work to keep Hoosiers safe during the coronavirus outbreak, we want to ensure that you have the most recent updates to keep you informed.

**In the News**

**[Gov. Eric Holcomb and State Health Commissioner Dr. Kris Box sit down with reporters Friday in Indianapolis to talk about the coronavirus](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=fc2b037fe2eb4397bc0370e27415e90a&_e=-QE9tb7c-N0czqVsNohxewJbH9itjn8uo-WSYJ2kWa99B5lFzmcJchOVb8gEhHsKVh1m7B_fOQB8K4MT3-zMQiSVrHcJCS54NKh0r-zuzwgBep-wxM58RWfLWxSB-g494ra8n_bRz2UML3f-EMHw7ozgdcmdgrw6ul6wDppNJoPW_vpwmFpDejhWzF2sajdRU9ieGiXqHspvs87i2WYeXqKvmFR2RceDuWF_EXIIE4Z5qwr7lZ3XVCoU665wZjofLrr4xBzNky3ei51WvkTm7JUHkQBEo-ng5aQAwbNqa4I639nVfBTwIfiJ0wSazNNM)**

**[One-on-one interview: Gov. Eric Holcomb discusses coronavirus pandemic, ‘new normal’ for Hoosiers](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=fc2b037fe2eb4397bc0370e27415e90a&_e=-QE9tb7c-N0czqVsNohxewJsmHI58qJgSykH0YutLOTagyKJCKjaLeHbvn_UkGT5xGTThikB1AxDZ0InHNldE3Kb515GwpyNPOd1DVAxhdXxi9z5F8Q8snGIzrCFFWhz3CUH8zDQdCP-AqFte6ciVnX0Iqs1Md4pPtur8J6Ln_MUg6Aq2t7vd5qPzrc-lHlsV69NxEfEU-b_DC4E47Izbuv6xQ6Q_J372KXWwZzS7athSFzn43TclXyo4EzTmn0T8LVri7h1w5n2mE-2VWTxooit3uKSK1EzjdntVOg2wlG6rZ6h7yfCtgkACc0-pIh2xz-3itsN_u8DdsErHpJH8pjg8xnviL_IxEaWaN7Ko5ueh7AgLl4Gug3NQLZYuHbCWNnkdIhB0883rvnJOI5xWA%3D%3D)**

**From the Governor's Desk
**

Governor Holcomb announced additional steps that are being taken to protect Hoosiers impacted by the coronavirus.

* All state agencies are evaluating rules and regulations that should be suspended or modified to assist Hoosiers during this public emergency.
* Governor Holcomb signed an executive order to lift regulations on commercial vehicles transporting goods to retailers, some of which are running short on supplies.
* Hoosiers who need to renew their Medicaid eligibility will get more time to complete the process. No services will be interrupted.
* Family Social Services Administration (FSSA) has asked federal officials to approve a request to temporarily waive the renewal process for Hoosiers who need SNAP or TANF benefits.
* Hoosiers on Medicaid will not pay co-pays for COVID-19 testing.
* Hoosiers on Medicaid can get 90-day refills of medication for chronic conditions.
* State officials are collaborating with the Indiana Department of Education to discuss solutions regarding student assessments and meals for children whose schools have closed.
* FSSA has given daycares specific guidance to protect children in their care.
* Community meals for senior citizens are being converted to home meal deliveries. Local partners, such as Area Agencies on Aging, have been given funding flexibility to cover the added costs of delivering meals. Thousands of meals are being served daily.
* Every community has a child care resource and referral agency to connect parents with local child care options and provide referrals for support. In the News From the Governor's Desk Governor Holcomb announced additional steps that are being taken to protect Hoosiers impacted by the coronavirus. All state agencies are evaluating rules and regulations that should be suspended or modified to assist Hoosiers during this public emergency. Governor Holcomb signed an executive order to lift regulations on commercial vehicles transporting goods to retailers, some of which are running short on supplies. Hoosiers who need to renew their Medicaid eligibility will get more time to complete the process. No services will be interrupted. Family Social Services Administration (FSSA) has asked federal officials to approve a request to temporarily waive the renewal process for Hoosiers who need SNAP or TANF benefits. Hoosiers on Medicaid will not pay co-pays for COVID-19 testing. Hoosiers on Medicaid can get 90-day refills of medication for chronic conditions. State officials are collaborating with the Indiana Department of Education to discuss solutions regarding student assessments and meals for children whose schools have closed. FSSA has given daycares specific guidance to protect children in their care. Community meals for senior citizens are being converted to home meal deliveries. Hello

I can't thank you enough for all you've done so far and all the volunteers who stepped up, voiced their opinion, and spoke on behalf of our campaign at each county convention yesterday. I'm humbled by your support and encouragement and I know that together we will win this election.

I also saw thousands of Republicans yesterday come together to craft the platform of our party. We need leaders in Congress who will turn the values we place in our platform into action and always put the voice of grassroots conservatives first.

We need more citizen legislators in DC who will keep their promises, uphold the Constitution and rule of law, and use our platform as a checklist for getting things done. It's conservative outsiders like your neighbor that will champion the causes we care about.

But I can't do it all alone. Get involved with our campaign to change politics in DC before DC changes us
⤵️ (https://emojipedia.org/right-arrow-curving-down/?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489)
HOST A GATHERING (http://richardsforcongress.com/volunteer?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489)
KNOCK DOORS (http://richardsforcongress.com/volunteer?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489)
LIKE US ON FACEBOOK (http://facebook.com/bretrichards4congress?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489)
PUT UP A SIGN (http://richardsforcongress.com/volunteer?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489)
Contribute $10 (https://richardsforcongress.com/contribution/?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489)
As always, get in touch with me at bret@richardsforcongress.com (mailto:bret@richardsforcongress.com) or (712)-579-4753 to tell me about your vision for Iowa or to lend a hand on our campaign. I look forward to hearing from you.

Please read on to see what I've been up to this week and all of the things we will accomplish together in the coming months.

I appreciate your continued support.

Your neighbor,
p.s. 79 DAYS until the primary on June 2nd. We NEED your support (http://richardsforcongress.com/contribution?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489) today!
BUILDING OUR NETWORK
Proud to witness the grassroots of our party at four county conventions in the district. Heard from and spoke at Butler County (https://www.facebook.com/Butler-County-Iowa-GOP-880918318692381/?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489) in Allison to Floyd County (https://www.facebook.com/Floyd-County-Iowa-GOP-2008922476058207/?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489) in Rockford to Hamilton County (https://www.facebook.com/Hamilton-County-Republican-Central-Committee-193985027321468?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489) in Webster City and Humboldt County (https://www.facebook.com/Official-Humboldt-County-Iowa-GOP-1615630805375022/?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489) in Humboldt. Great to be with local conservatives at the Webster County GOP (https://www.facebook.com/webstercountygop/?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489) in Fort Dodge and the Chickasaw County GOP (https://www.facebook.com/ChickasawCountyRepublicans/?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489) in New Hampton as well.

LETTER OF SUPPORT

Colby Drey, Sioux City: "Bret would not be beholden to the political establishment in Des Moines or the special interests in Washington, D.C. He would be accountable to his Iowa neighbors whose values he has lived out in his daily life." READ MORE (https://richardsforcongress.com/letter-support-bret-richards-for-4th-district-u-s-house-seat/?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489)

SUBMIT YOUR OWN LETTER TO THE EDITOR (http://richardsforcongress.com/volunteer?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489)
RECENT COVERAGE

https://richardsforcongress.com/richards-talks-rural-prosperity-grassroots-support-and-running-to-be-a-citizen-legislator-with-klga/?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489

http://www.algonaradio.com/2020/03/challenger-to-king-stops-in-algona/?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489
"In Iowa, we can sit down over coffee in 20 minutes and solve some of these problems that politicians have been talking about for 40 years. If we start sending a different type of person—someone that cares more about the country than a career—we can make those changes."
UP NEXT

Looking forward to meeting with young conservatives in Fort Dodge, local farmers at the Chickasaw County Farm Bureau (https://www.facebook.com/ChickasawCountyFarmBureau/?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489) in New Hampton, and conservative activists at a get together in Sioux City.

STAY INFORMED

GET INVOLVED

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O5xPeOhUV0U&utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=6f228f4c73-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-6f228f4c73-25822489

body:
Governor Holcomb has taken big steps to protect Hoosiers during the coronavirus outbreak, and he's working hard to keep Hoosiers informed during... |
---
| |
---
| **In the News: Gov. Holcomb Leading Indiana's Coronavirus Response**
---
| Governor Holcomb has taken big steps to protect Hoosiers during the coronavirus outbreak, and he's working hard to keep Hoosiers up to date on the latest developments. He did several one-on-one news interviews with outlets throughout the state last week, and we wanted to make sure you saw them.

**There's lots of important information here. Once you've read and watched some of these stories, please consider sharing them on social media and forwarding this email or individual stories to your friends, family and fellow Hoosiers.**
* [WFLI: The Message from Governor Holcomb: We Are All In This Together](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=31f53ca66f&e=1acd9bb3d6)
* [Fort Wayne Journal Gazette: Holcomb: Hoosiers Living in New Normal](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=94fbc03fd1&e=1acd9bb3d6)
* [Fox 59: Gov. Eric Holcomb Discusses Coronavirus Pandemic, ‘New Normal’ for Hoosiers](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=fa3b9057d7&e=1acd9bb3d6)
* [RTV6: Gov. Holcomb takes additional steps to fight COVID-19 in Indiana](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ce30e420e1&e=1acd9bb3d6)
* [WPTA 21: ‘It’s All Hands on Deck’: Gov. Holcomb Talks Novel Coronavirus in Sit-Down Interview](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d59f21f5ed&e=1acd9bb3d6)
* [WISH-TV: Indiana Gov. Holcomb, Health Commissioner Dr. Kris Box Talk with News 8 About Coronavirus](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=863f54570b&e=1acd9bb3d6)
* [Associated Press: Indiana Governor Letting School Districts Decide on Closings](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=654b94e629&e=1acd9bb3d6)
